stages.label: Newborn foundation
stages.summary: Focus on milk intake rhythm, caregiver confidence, and early feeding cues.

reminderTemplates.title: Build a calm milk rhythm
reminderTemplates.body: At this stage, consistent milk feeds and clear logs help you notice patterns without overthinking every feed.
reminderTemplates.whyThisMatters: Newborn feeding patterns shift quickly, so a simple rhythm matters more than variety.
actions: Watch for satiety and hunger cues

stages.label: Starting solids
stages.summary: Support first solids with iron-rich foods, simple variety, and gentle observation.

reminderTemplates.title: Keep iron and variety in view
reminderTemplates.body: This is a good stage to introduce simple iron-rich foods and keep meals low-pressure while your baby learns.
reminderTemplates.whyThisMatters: Early solids are more about repeated exposure and iron support than large amounts eaten.
actions: Offer one clear iron-rich food this week
Log new foods so patterns stay visible

stages.label: Texture building
stages.summary: Expand texture range and food diversity while watching repeated gaps.

reminderTemplates.title: Build texture confidence
reminderTemplates.body: Your baby may be ready for more texture practice now, along with steadier variety across the week.
reminderTemplates.whyThisMatters: Repeated exposure to different foods and textures supports confidence without needing perfect meals every day.
actions: Repeat familiar foods in new textures
Offer fruit or vegetables across several days
Keep iron-rich foods in the weekly rotation

stages.label: Shared table transition
stages.summary: Move toward family-style eating patterns with steadier variety and safe table habits.

reminderTemplates.title: Shape a steady table routine
reminderTemplates.body: This stage is a good time to reinforce family mealtime rhythm and keep variety steady across the week.
reminderTemplates.whyThisMatters: Patterns across the week matter more now than any single meal.
actions: Keep protein and iron-rich foods in rotation
Use family meals to model eating variety
